back_translation: And livestock — many — was for the sons of Reuben and for the sons of Gad, exceedingly numerous; and they saw the land of Jazer and the land of Gilead, and behold, the place was a place for livestock.
And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben came, and spoke to Moses and to Eleazar the priest and to the leaders of the congregation, saying:
'Ataroth and Dibon and Jazer and Nimrah and Heshbon and Elealeh, and Sebam and Nebo and Beon.'
'The land which the LORD struck before the congregation of Israel is a land of livestock; and your servants have livestock.'
And they said: 'If we have found favor in your eyes, let this land be given to your servants as a possession; do not make us cross the Jordan.'
And Moses said to the sons of Gad and to the sons of Reuben: 'Shall your brothers go to war and you sit here?'
'And why do you discourage the heart of the sons of Israel from crossing to the land which the LORD has given to them?'
'Thus your fathers did, when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to see the land.'
'And they went up as far as the Valley of Eshcol, and saw the land, and discouraged the heart of the sons of Israel — not to come to the land which the LORD had given to them.'
'And the nose (anger) of the LORD burned on that day, and He swore, saying:'
'If the men coming up from Egypt, from a son of twenty years and upward, shall see the land which I swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob — for they did not follow Me fully —'
'except Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite, and Joshua son of Nun, for they followed after the LORD fully.'
'And the nose (anger) of the LORD burned against Israel, and He made them wander in the wilderness forty years, until the whole generation, doing evil in the eyes of the LORD, was consumed.'
'And behold, you have risen up in place of your fathers — a brood of sinful men — to add still more to the burning anger of the nose of the LORD against Israel.'
'For if you turn back from following Him, He will yet again leave him in the wilderness, and you will destroy all this people.'
And they approached him and said: 'Folds of sheep we will build for our livestock here, and cities for our little ones.'

'And we — we will equip ourselves armed before the sons of Israel, until we bring them to their place; and our little ones shall dwell in the fortified cities from before the inhabitants of the land.'
'We will not return to our houses until the sons of Israel have inherited, each his inheritance.'
'For we will not inherit with them across the Jordan and beyond, because our inheritance has come to us from across the Jordan, eastward.'
And Moses said to them: 'If you do this thing, if you arm yourselves before the LORD for war.'
'And every armed man of you crosses the Jordan before the LORD, until He has driven out His enemies from His presence.'

'And the land is subdued before the LORD, then afterward you shall return, and you shall be innocent before the LORD and before Israel; and this land shall be for you as a possession before the LORD.'
'But if you will not do so, behold, you have sinned against the LORD; and know your sin which will find you.'
'Build for yourselves cities for your little ones and folds for your flocks, and what comes out from your mouth you shall do.'
And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben said to Moses, saying: 'Your servants will do as my lord commands.'
'Our little ones, our wives, our livestock, and all our cattle shall be there in the cities of Gilead.'
'And your servants will cross over, every armed man of the army, before the LORD for war, as my lord speaks.'
And Moses commanded concerning them — Eleazar the priest, and Joshua son of Nun, and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the sons of Israel.

And Moses said to them: 'If the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben cross the Jordan with you, every armed man for war before the LORD, and the land is subdued before you, then you shall give to them the land of Gilead as a possession.'
'But if they do not cross over armed with you, then they shall be given a possession in your midst in the land of Canaan.'
And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben answered, saying: 'What the LORD has spoken to your servants, so we will do.'
'We — we will cross over armed before the LORD into the land of Canaan, and with us the possession of our inheritance shall be from across the Jordan.'

And Moses gave to them — to the sons of Gad, and to the sons of Reuben, and to the half of the tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph — the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorite, and the kingdom of Og king of Bashan: the land, by its cities, in the territories — cities of the land round about.
And the sons of Gad built Dibon, and Ataroth, and Aroer.
And Atroth-shophan, and Jazer, and Jogbehah.
And Beth-nimrah, and Beth-haran — fortified cities, and folds of sheep.
And the sons of Reuben built Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Kiriathaim.
And Nebo, and Baal-meon — names changed — and Sibmah; and they called by names the names of the cities which they built.
And the sons of Machir son of Manasseh went to Gilead, and captured it; and he dispossessed the Amorite who was in it.
And Moses gave Gilead to Machir son of Manasseh, and he dwelt in it.
And Jair son of Manasseh went, and captured their villages, and called them Havvoth-jair.
And Nobah went, and captured Kenath and its villages, and called it Nobah, by his name.
